annotations.label: Isomorphism

anchor.snippet: an isomorphism is a structure-preserving mapping or morphism between two structures of the same type that can be reversed by an inverse mapping

annotations.note: The general categorical notion is captured by `CategoryTheory.Iso`; concrete instances (MulEquiv, RingEquiv, LinearEquiv, etc.) realize it for specific structure types.

annotations.label: Isomorphic structures

anchor.snippet: Two mathematical structures are isomorphic if an isomorphism exists between them

annotations.note: `IsIsomorphic X Y := Nonempty (X ≅ Y)` directly formalizes the relation of two objects being isomorphic.

annotations.label: Automorphism

anchor.snippet: An automorphism is an isomorphism from a structure to itself

annotations.note: Automorphisms appear generally as `Aut X := X ≅ X` in category theory, and as `Equiv.Perm`, `MulAut`, `RingAut`, etc. for specific structures.

annotations.label: Canonical isomorphism

anchor.snippet: An isomorphism between two structures is a canonical isomorphism

annotations.note: Canonicity is a meta/informal notion (often realized via universal properties) and is not formalized as a single definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homomorphism is isomorphism iff bijective

anchor.snippet: a homomorphism is an isomorphism if and only if it is bijective

annotations.note: Mathlib provides `MulEquiv.ofBijective`, `RingEquiv.ofBijective`, etc. for each algebraic category, but there is no single statement covering all algebraic categories simultaneously.

annotations.label: Isometry

anchor.snippet: An isometry is an isomorphism of metric spaces

annotations.note: `Isometry` and bijective variant `IsometryEquiv` formalize distance-preserving (iso)morphisms of pseudo-metric spaces.

annotations.label: Homeomorphism

anchor.snippet: A homeomorphism is an isomorphism of topological spaces

annotations.note: `Homeomorph` is the bundled isomorphism of topological spaces.

annotations.label: Diffeomorphism

anchor.snippet: A diffeomorphism is an isomorphism of spaces equipped with a differential structure

annotations.note: `Diffeomorph` formalizes smooth bijections with smooth inverse between manifolds.

annotations.label: Symplectomorphism

anchor.snippet: A symplectomorphism is an isomorphism of symplectic manifolds

annotations.note: No declaration matching `Symplectomorph` (or symplectic-form-preserving diffeomorphism) was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Permutation

anchor.snippet: A permutation is an automorphism of a set

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm α := α ≃ α` is the type of permutations of a set.

annotations.label: Logarithm and exponential as group isomorphisms

anchor.snippet: are group isomorphisms that are inverse of each other

annotations.note: `Real.expOrderIso` packages exp/log as an order isomorphism ℝ ≃o ℝ>0, but a bundled `MulEquiv` between the additive reals and multiplicative positive reals via exp/log is not directly present.

annotations.label: Integers modulo 6 ring isomorphism

anchor.snippet: These rings are isomorphic under the following map

annotations.note: `ZMod.chineseRemainder` gives the ring isomorphism `ZMod (m*n) ≃+* ZMod m × ZMod n` for coprime m,n, specializing to ZMod 6 ≃+* ZMod 2 × ZMod 3.

annotations.label: Chinese remainder theorem (special case)

anchor.snippet: This is a special case of the Chinese remainder theorem

annotations.note: `ZMod.chineseRemainder` formalizes the CRT statement; a more general ideal-theoretic version exists in `Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Quotient.Operations`.

annotations.label: Relation-preserving isomorphism

anchor.snippet: then an isomorphism from X to Y is a bijective function

annotations.note: `RelIso r s` (notation `r ≃r s`) is exactly an equivalence f such that `s (f a) (f b) ↔ r a b`.

annotations.label: Relation properties preserved by isomorphism

anchor.snippet: S is reflexive , irreflexive , symmetric , antisymmetric , asymmetric , transitive , total , trichotomous

annotations.note: Individual `RelIso`/`OrderIso` lemmas show preservation of specific properties (reflexive, transitive, etc.), but there is no single bundled statement enumerating all of them.

annotations.label: Order isomorphism

anchor.snippet: Such an isomorphism is called an order isomorphism

annotations.note: `OrderIso α β` (notation `α ≃o β`) formalizes order isomorphisms as `RelIso` for the `≤` relation.

annotations.label: Relation-preserving automorphism

anchor.snippet: then this is a relation-preserving automorphism

annotations.note: Self-relations are handled as `RelIso r r` / `OrderIso α α`; no dedicated `RelAut` type exists, though `OrderAut`-style usages appear via these specializations.

annotations.label: Graph isomorphism

anchor.snippet: an isomorphism between two graphs G and H is a bijective map f from the vertices of G to the vertices of H that preserves the "edge structure"

annotations.note: `SimpleGraph.Iso G H` is defined as a `RelIso G.Adj H.Adj`, i.e. a vertex bijection preserving the adjacency relation.

annotations.label: Order isomorphism between posets

anchor.snippet: an isomorphism between two partially ordered sets P and Q is a bijective map

annotations.note: `OrderIso` directly formalizes bijections between posets that preserve and reflect ≤.

annotations.label: Whole numbers vs blood types ordering

anchor.snippet: the set {1,2,3,6} of whole numbers ordered by the is-a-factor-of relation is isomorphic to the set

annotations.note: This specific four-element divisibility/subset poset example is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hilbert space isomorphism

anchor.snippet: an isomorphism between two Hilbert spaces is a bijection preserving addition, scalar multiplication, and inner product

annotations.note: `LinearIsometryEquiv` formalizes norm-preserving linear equivalences; on inner product spaces, norm preservation is equivalent to inner-product preservation via polarization.

annotations.label: Good regulator theorem

anchor.snippet: the good regulator theorem or Conant–Ashby theorem is stated as "Every good regulator of a system must be a model of that system"

annotations.note: The Conant–Ashby good regulator theorem from cybernetics has no formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isomorphism in a category

anchor.snippet: given a category C , an isomorphism is a morphism

annotations.note: `CategoryTheory.Iso X Y` bundles a morphism with a two-sided inverse, exactly the categorical definition.

annotations.label: Isomorphic categories

anchor.snippet: Two categories C and D are isomorphic if there exist functors

annotations.note: Mathlib emphasizes `CategoryTheory.Equivalence` (functors with natural iso to identity); strict isomorphism of categories appears as `Iso` in `Cat` but the weaker notion is standard.

annotations.label: Isomorphism vs bijective morphism in concrete categories

anchor.snippet: an isomorphism must be bijective on the underlying sets

annotations.note: Mathlib has the iff-bijective fact in `Type` and for many concrete categories, but no single general statement handles all concrete categories at once.

annotations.label: Isomorphism is an equivalence relation

anchor.snippet: the relation that two mathematical objects are isomorphic is an equivalence relation

annotations.note: `isIsomorphicSetoid C` packages `IsIsomorphic` as an equivalence relation on the objects of a category.

annotations.label: Isomorphism class

anchor.snippet: An equivalence class given by isomorphisms is commonly called an isomorphism class

annotations.note: `isomorphismClasses` sends a category to the quotient of its objects by `isIsomorphicSetoid`.

annotations.label: Isomorphism classes of sets

anchor.snippet: Two sets are isomorphic if there is a bijection between them

annotations.note: `Cardinal` is defined as the quotient of types by equipotence, and `Cardinal.eq` exactly says two types have the same cardinal iff there is a bijection.

annotations.label: Isomorphism class of finite-dimensional vector space

anchor.snippet: The isomorphism class of a finite-dimensional vector space can be identified with the non-negative integer representing its dimension

annotations.note: `LinearEquiv.ofFinrankEq` (and `nonempty_linearEquiv_of_finrank_eq`) shows finite-dimensional vector spaces are linearly isomorphic iff their `finrank` agrees.

annotations.label: Classification of finite simple groups

anchor.snippet: The classification of finite simple groups enumerates the isomorphism classes of all finite simple groups

annotations.note: The CFSG is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classification of closed surfaces

anchor.snippet: The classification of closed surfaces enumerates the isomorphism classes of all connected closed surfaces

annotations.note: No classification of closed surfaces is stated in Mathlib (file `PoincareConjecture.lean` only references it informally).

annotations.label: Ordinals as isomorphism classes of well-ordered sets

anchor.snippet: Ordinals intuitively correspond to isomorphism classes of well-ordered sets

annotations.note: `Ordinal` is defined as the quotient of `WellOrder` by order-isomorphism via `Ordinal.isEquivalent`.

annotations.label: Planar subalgebras of M(2,R)

anchor.snippet: There are three isomorphism classes of the planar subalgebras

annotations.note: The classification of 2-dimensional ℝ-subalgebras of M(2,ℝ) (split-complex, dual, complex) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fundamental group base point dependence

anchor.snippet: the fundamental group of a space

annotations.note: `FundamentalGroup` is defined for a pointed space and the fundamental groupoid yields isomorphisms between fundamental groups at different basepoints in a path-connected space, though the explicit base-change isomorphism statement isn't a single named lemma.

annotations.label: Equality vs isomorphism

anchor.snippet: one must distinguish equality and isomorphism

annotations.note: The philosophical distinction between equality and isomorphism is not formalized as a proposition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Integers and even numbers as isomorphic ordered sets

anchor.snippet: integers and even numbers are isomorphic as ordered sets and abelian groups

annotations.note: No specific declaration formalizes the isomorphism ℤ ≃ 2ℤ as ordered group; the doubling map exists implicitly but is not stated as a named iso.

annotations.label: Universal property gives unique isomorphism of rationals

anchor.snippet: Given two fields with these properties, there is a unique field isomorphism between them

annotations.note: Mathlib has unique ring homomorphisms from ℚ into any characteristic-zero field via `Rat.castRingHom` and `RingHom.ext_rat`, capturing ℚ's initial-object property among characteristic-zero fields, but a stand-alone universal-property uniqueness theorem isn't packaged.
